HomeHeader.description: Let’s be real: <bold>AI is already writing the code you’re trying to learn.</bold> While you’re dreaming of a six-figure salary, the market is preparing you for <bold>minimum wage entry roles</bold>. We skip the fluff. From the drudgery of <bold>QA Fundamentals</bold> to the math-heavy hell of <bold>Three.js</bold>, this is a survival guide for the <italic>Post-Junior era</italic>.<block>Build what machines cannot imagine, or get replaced by a prompt.</block>

Footer.description: A project by <bold>Wiktor EXY. Kowalczyk</bold>. Built with <italic>Next.js</italic> and <italic>GSAP</italic>.

NotFound.description: The route you are trying to access does not exist in this reality. Either the URL is a hallucination, or we haven't engineered this path yet.

qa.label: 01. Quality Assurance
qa.quote: "It's not about finding bugs. It's about being the only person in the room brave enough to ruin everyone's mood."
qa.description: The reality of QA is 80% detective work on a bug that no one else can reproduce, and 20% fighting for quality.

frontend.label: 02. Frontend Development
frontend.quote: "You are a sapper in the minefield of browsers. A single margin in Safari can blow up your entire Grid."
frontend.description: Frontend is an eternal struggle between the designer's vision, API limitations, and the reality of cross-browser compatibility.

threeD.label: 03. 3D & WebGL
threeD.quote: "In 3D, debugging is pure geometry. If you mess up a vector, your object simply ceases to exist in our reality."
threeD.description: Matrices, quaternions, and shaders are the only things standing between a blank screen and a photorealistic world.

reality.label: Final Chapter: Reality Check
reality.dreamQuote: "You will build innovative products and change the world in a modern office."
reality.realityQuote: "You will debug code written 5 years ago and explain why the estimate was wrong."

header.title: QA Fundamentals
header.subtitle: Mastering the art of breaking things to make them unbreakable. Logic, edge cases, and quality mindset.

mindset.label: 01 / The Skeptic
mindset.tagline: Testing is not a phase; it's a pathological distrust of 'it works on my machine'.
mindset.quote: "A QA engineer doesn't walk into a bar to order a beer. They order 1 beer, 0 beers, 999999999 beers, a lizard, and -1 beers."

edgeCases.title: Edge Cases
edgeCases.desc: Hunting for the 1% scenario that crashes the system. We don't care about the happy path; we care about what happens when the user loses Wi-Fi while submitting a credit card form.

criticalThinking.title: Critical Thinking
criticalThinking.desc: Analyzing requirements to find contradictions before a single line of code is written. QA starts at the documentation level.

anatomy.label: 02 / UI
anatomy.tagline: Learning to see the invisible structure of the modern web.

item1.title: The Box Model & Spacing
item1.desc: Padding vs. Margin. We check if the visual rhythm is consistent. 16px is not 15px. Inconsistent spacing is the first sign of a low-quality build.

item2.title: Component States
item2.desc: Hover, Focus, Active, Disabled, Loading. A button is not just a rectangle; it's a multi-state machine that must remain accessible to screen readers.

item3.title: Typography & Hierarchy
item3.desc: Font-weights, line-heights, and contrast ratios. We verify if the text is legible across all devices and follows WCAG accessibility standards.

toolkit.label: 03 / Technical
toolkit.tagline: The instruments we use to dissect, document, and debug reality.

table.row1: Inspecting pixel values and design specs.
table.row2: Managing the bug lifecycle and sprint progress.
table.row3: Monitoring console errors and network latency.

human.label: 04 / Human
human.tagline: Software is built by people. Quality is maintained through communication.

designers.title: Working with Designers
designers.desc: You are the guardian of their vision. When a developer says "it's too hard to implement," you find the middle ground that preserves the user experience.

developers.title: Working with Developers
developers.desc: Don't just report bugs—report solutions. Provide logs, reproduction steps, and screen recordings to make their fixing process as painless as possible.

academy.label: The Academy
academy.tagline: Theoretical knowledge is a liability without practice. Master the QA craft in the Post-Junior era.

step1.title: The Lexicon
step1.desc: Terminology and visual evidence of UI components.

step2.title: Evidence Room
step2.desc: Visual bug hunting in simulated environments.

header.title: Modern Frontend
header.subtitle: Architecting scalable, accessible, and performant user interfaces with React and Tailwind CSS.

mindset.label: 01 / The Browser
mindset.tagline: Frontend is not just about making things look pretty; it's about handling chaos across a thousand different viewports.
mindset.quote: "Your code is an educated guess about how a browser in another part of the world will interpret a set of instructions."

safari.title: The Safari Tax
safari.desc: Debugging the specific CSS quirks of the modern Internet Explorer. Everything works until you open it on an iPhone.

state.title: State of Mind
state.desc: Managing how data flows through a complex interface. When one button click triggers five different UI updates, you need a solid architectural plan.

anatomy.label: 02 / Component
anatomy.tagline: Breaking the UI into reusable, atomic pieces that manage their own behavior.

item1.title: JSX & The DOM
item1.desc: Writing HTML-like structures inside JavaScript. It's about bridging the gap between content and logic, creating a dynamic tree of elements that react to user input.

item2.title: Props & Hooks
item2.desc: The nervous system of React. Passing data down and hooking into the component lifecycle to fetch data, manage effects, or store local state.

item3.title: Responsive Grid & Flex
item3.desc: Using Tailwind's utility-first approach to build fluid layouts. Implementing complex designs that remain balanced from 320px to 4K monitors.

pipeline.label: 03 / Dev
pipeline.tagline: The stack that powers the modern developer's workflow.

table.row1: Handling routing, SSR, and server actions.
table.row2: Atomic styling without leaving the HTML.

bridge.label: 04 / The Bridge
bridge.tagline: Frontend is the intersection of design, backend, and the end-user.

api.title: API Integration
api.desc: Consuming JSON from the backend and transforming it into a human-readable interface. Handling loading states and errors gracefully is a core skill.

a11y.title: Accessibility (a11y)
a11y.desc: Building for everyone. Using semantic HTML and ARIA labels to ensure that the web is usable for people with disabilities or screen readers.

academy.label: The Academy
academy.tagline: Syntax is easy; architecture is hard. Step into the lab to master the art of building interfaces that don't crumble under pressure.

step1.title: Component Lexicon
step1.desc: From Props and State to Higher-Order Components. The vocabulary of React.

step2.title: Execution Room
step2.desc: Real-time state management puzzles. Logic drills that go beyond Todo apps.

step3.title: Performance Lab
step3.desc: Analyzing render cycles and Lighthouse scores. Make it fast or make it over.

header.title: Three.js & WebGL
header.subtitle: Pushing the boundaries of the browser. Shaders, raycasting, and immersive 3D environments.

math.label: 01 / Vector
math.tagline: High school geometry is no longer a memory—it is the law. In 3D, if you can't calculate it, it doesn't exist.
math.quote: "Matrices and quaternions are the invisible scaffolding of your world. One wrong digit and your reality collapses."

linear.title: Linear Algebra
linear.desc: Mastering Dot products, Cross products, and Matrix transformations. Everything from camera movement to light reflection depends on these calculations.

coords.title: Coordinate Systems
coords.desc: Navigating the difference between Local, World, and Screen space. Mapping a 2D mouse click to a 3D object is the first "magic" trick you'll master.

pipeline.label: 02 / The
pipeline.tagline: Moving from the CPU to the GPU. Learning to talk directly to the graphics card.

vertex.title: Vertex Shaders
vertex.desc: Manipulating the position of every single point in your geometry. This is how we create procedural waves, wind blowing through grass, or morphing shapes.

fragment.title: Fragment Shaders
fragment.desc: Calculating the color of every pixel on the screen. From photorealistic lighting to glitch effects and generative noise patterns. This is pure GLSL math.

raycasting.title: Raycasting
raycasting.desc: Firing invisible lasers from the camera to detect intersections. Essential for hovering over objects, clicking buttons in 3D, and simple physics.

stack.label: 03 / The
stack.tagline: The industry-standard ecosystem for building web-based immersion.

table.row1: The abstraction layer for WebGL. Scenes, Lights, Meshes.
table.row3: Exporting GLTF/GLB assets and baking textures.

optimization.label: 04 / Optimization
optimization.tagline: The GPU is powerful, but it has limits. 60 FPS is not a suggestion—it's a requirement.

drawCalls.title: Draw Calls
drawCalls.desc: Every object is a request to the GPU. Learning to batch geometries and instantiate meshes to keep the frame rate stable on mobile devices.

baking.title: Texture Baking
baking.desc: Fake it 'til you make it. Calculating lights in Blender and "painting" them onto textures to avoid expensive real-time shadow calculations in the browser.

academy.label: The Academy
academy.tagline: Geometry is the language of reality. Step into the laboratory to master the vectors, matrices, and shaders that build immersive worlds.

step1.title: Geometry Lexicon
step1.desc: Normals, UVs, and Matrices. The vocabulary of the 3rd dimension.

step2.title: Shader Pipeline
step2.desc: Vertex and Fragment logic drills. Writing GLSL noise patterns.

step3.title: Scene Architecture
step3.desc: Managing lights and R3F lifecycle for immersive experiences.

header.title: Reality Check
header.subtitle: The unfiltered truth about the IT ecosystem. Decoding the daily grind in startups and the strategic layers of corporations.

hype.label: 01 / The
hype.tagline: Marketing for bootcamps is not reality. The gold rush is over; the era of competence has begun.
hype.quote: "You were told it's about ping-pong tables and free coffee. It's actually about staring at a terminal for 6 hours only to find a missing comma."

dream.title: The Dream
dream.desc: Building innovative startups in glass skyscrapers, disrupting industries, and retiring at 30 with a passive income stream.

reality.title: The Reality
reality.desc: Maintaining a legacy PHP system for a local insurance company while attending four meetings a day that could have been an email.

ai.label: 02 / The AI
ai.tagline: Machines are getting better at the 'how'. You must get better at the 'why'.

ghosting.title: Entry Level Ghosting
ghosting.desc: The junior market is saturated. AI can now write basic CRUD apps better and faster than a fresh graduate. To survive, you need to understand architecture, not just syntax.

trap.title: The Prompt Trap
trap.desc: Relying on AI to solve every bug will stunt your growth. If you don't know how the code works, you can't fix it when the AI hallucinations start.

vision.title: Critical Vision
vision.desc: "Build what machines cannot imagine." Human intuition, empathy for the user, and complex decision-making are your only true defenses against replacement.

market.label: 03 / Market
market.tagline: Choosing your battleground defines your mental health.

table.row1b: Feeling like a cog in a massive, slow machine.
table.row2b: Unpaid overtime and extreme burnout risk.
table.row3b: Being a salesman, accountant, and dev all at once.

human.label: 04 / Human
human.tagline: Soft skills are the hard skills of the 2020s.

comm.title: Communication over Code
comm.desc: Being able to explain why a feature will take 3 weeks instead of 3 days to a non-technical manager is more important than knowing how to center a div.

learn.title: Lifelong Unlearning
learn.desc: The framework you learn today will be obsolete in 24 months. Master the fundamentals, not the tools. Tools change; logic is eternal.

academy.label: The Academy
academy.tagline: Technical skills get you the interview; market intelligence gets you the job. Step into the lab to decode the hidden rules of the IT ecosystem.

step1.title: Corporate Lexicon
step1.desc: Decoding OKRs and KPIs. Speak the language of business stakeholders.

step2.title: The "ASAP" Drills
step2.desc: Negotiating timelines. How to say "no" while sounding like a "yes".

step3.title: The AI Shield
step3.desc: Optimizing for ATS filters. Making it through the machine gatekeepers.

footer.label: Manifesto End
footer.title: Now that you know the <purple>Truth</purple>, <break></break>will you still <u>Code?</u>

manifesto.title: Why does this journal exist?
manifesto.p1: The IT market is no longer a playground. The era where centering a div was enough to earn six figures is gone. Today, <bold>AI writes code better than the average junior</bold>, and companies are looking for engineers, not just coders.
manifesto.p2: The Frontend Journal is not another course. It's a record of experiences, a brutal analysis of reality, and a guide to technologies that actually matter: from QA logic, through React architecture, to the mathematical hell of 3D shaders.
manifesto.quote: "Build what machines cannot imagine, or be replaced by a prompt."

header.title: System Logs
header.subtitle: Evolution history of the Journal structure.

v120.desc: Ecosystem expansion: Integrated specialized sectors for Frontend, 3D (WebGL), and Reality Check. Refined header system, implemented sticky navigation, and optimized i18n for complex UI structures.

v110.desc: Full implementation of the multi-language system (Next-intl). Architecture ready for content scaling.

v100.desc: System core initialization. Deployment of design system, GSAP animations, and main layout.
